headlines: Allied Democratic Forces insurgency; 2021–2022 Democratic Republic of the Congo attacks
Russian occupation of Kherson, 2022 food crises

events.text: Russo-Ukrainian War
2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine
Battle of Donbas
Battle of Sievierodonetsk
Ukrainian authorities say that Russian forces have entered Sieverodonetsk, Luhansk Oblast, leaving the city without water.
Rebel forces say a Ukrainian shelling attack in Donetsk has killed five civilians (including a teenager) and left 13 injured.
A French journalist is killed by Russian shelling in Luhansk Oblast, according to the region's governor Serhiy Haidai.
A car bomb is detonated in Russian-occupied Melitopol, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, injuring at least three people. Russian authorities blame pro-Ukrainian residents for the incident.
Allied Democratic Forces insurgency; 2021–2022 Democratic Republic of the Congo attacks
Fifteen people are killed in a suspected Islamist attack by the Allied Democratic Forces in North Kivu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.
Insurgency in Southeastern Nigeria
Nigerian police say that unknown militants have kidnapped a prelate in Abia State.
Nigerian bandit conflict
Bandits kill up to 30 vigilantes in Zamfara State, Nigeria.
Russian occupation of Kherson, 2022 food crises

Russia begins exporting grain from the occupied Kherson Oblast in Southern Ukraine to Russia. Russian-occupying administration official Kirill Stremousov says that the administration was also "working on the supplies of sunflower seeds to local and Russian processing plants". Ukraine accuses Russia of stealing its grain.
International sanctions during the Russo-Ukrainian War

After weeks of deliberations, all of the European Union member states agree to impose an oil embargo on most oil imports from Russia (with the exception of that brought by pipelines), to be fully implemented by the end of the year, and cut off Sberbank from SWIFT.

A report by the Blue Marine Foundation found that fishing vessels from the European Union were illegally harvesting fish in the exclusive economic zones off the coasts of India, Somalia and Mozambique even as the stocks of yellowfin tuna were on the verge of collapse.
Hurricane Agatha
One person is killed in Tapachula, Chiapas, as Hurricane Agatha approaches Mexico.
Five people are injured after a school bus crashes into a ditch and rolls over in Roztoka, Tarnów County, Poland.
The wreckage of the Cessna 182 that disappeared from radar the previous day is found near Broćanac, Croatia. All four people on board were killed.
Two people are killed and 20 others are injured after a car crashes into a crowd in Lincoln, Nebraska, United States.
2022 monkeypox outbreak
Thailand reports its first case of monkeypox.
Status of Jerusalem, Foreign relations of Israel, Foreign relations of Suriname
Surinamese Foreign Minister Albert Ramdin says that Suriname will open an embassy in Jerusalem.
Politics of Transnistria
Aleksandr Rozenberg is sworn as new Prime Minister of Transnistria.
2022 Australian federal election
2022 Liberal Party of Australia leadership election

Former Minister for Defence Peter Dutton is elected unopposed as Leader of the Liberal Party of Australia and, by extension, Leader of the Opposition following former Prime Minister Scott Morrison's resignation.
2022 National Party of Australia leadership spill

Former Deputy Prime Minister Barnaby Joyce is removed as Leader of the National Party of Australia in a leadership spill and replaced by his former Deputy and former Minister for Agriculture and Northern Australia David Littleproud.
As vote counting continues, the Australian Labor Party achieves a majority in the House of Representatives.
